## Pre-Submission Checklist

<!-- Mark completed items with an 'x' -->

### Project Structure
- [ ] Project is in the correct category folder
- [ ] Project uses `snake_case` naming convention
- [ ] Folder structure follows the guidelines

### Required Files
- [ ] `README.md` included and follows the [template](../.github/README_TEMPLATE.md)
- [ ] `requirements.txt` or `pyproject.toml` included
- [ ] `.env.example` included (if API keys are needed)
- [ ] `.gitignore` includes `.env` and sensitive files

### Documentation
- [ ] README has all required sections
- [ ] Installation steps are clear and tested
- [ ] Usage examples are provided
- [ ] Environment variables are documented
- [ ] Tech stack is clearly listed

### Code Quality
- [ ] Code follows Python best practices (PEP 8)
- [ ] No API keys or secrets in code
- [ ] Error handling is implemented
- [ ] Code includes helpful comments

### Testing
- [ ] Project has been tested locally
- [ ] All dependencies install correctly
- [ ] Application runs without errors
- [ ] Example use cases work as documented

### Git
- [ ] Only one project per PR (if adding multiple, split into separate PRs)
- [ ] Commit messages are clear and descriptive
- [ ] No unnecessary files included (e.g., `__pycache__`, `.DS_Store`)

## How to Test

<!-- Provide step-by-step instructions for reviewers to test your project -->

1. Clone the repo and checkout this branch
   ```bash
   git checkout [branch-name]
   ```

2. Navigate to the project directory
   ```bash
   cd [category]/[project-name]
   ```

3. Install dependencies
   ```bash
   pip install -r requirements.txt
   ```

4. Set up environment variables
   ```bash
   cp .env.example .env
   # Add your API keys to .env
   ```

5. Run the project
   ```bash
   [command to run the project]
   ```

6. Expected behavior:
   <!-- Describe what should happen when the project runs successfully -->
